title: Multimodal RAG for Documents, Images, Tables, Layout, and Audio · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed retrieval review reconstructs multimodal rag for documents, images, tables, layout, and audio from source, document, chunk, model, index, query, candidate, response, citation, evaluation, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then attacks one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for accessible multimodal evidence retriever after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.
Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.
Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review RAG engineering team handles case 1696 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide structured text alternatives and keyboard inspection and evaluate retrieval, grounding, citations, bias, injection, latency, and user comprehension by modality. Artifact under review: accessible multimodal evidence retriever.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval record says accessible multimodal evidence retriever looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof. This does not satisfy Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval for accessible multimodal evidence retriever.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide structured text alternatives and keyboard inspection and evaluate retrieval, grounding, citations, bias, injection, latency, and user comprehension by modality.
